cards.front: What is a Sequential Search algorithm?
cards.back: A Sequential Search algorithm checks each element of a list or array one by one until it finds a match, resulting in a time complexity of O(n).

cards.front: What is a Brute Force algorithm in string matching?
cards.back: A Brute Force algorithm in string matching is an exhaustive search method that checks every possible substring of the text and the pattern to find a match, resulting in a time complexity of O(n*m), where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Sequential Search algorithm for string matching?
cards.back: The time complexity of the Sequential Search algorithm for string matching is O(n*m), where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'bad character' optimization in Brute Force algorithm?
cards.back: The 'bad character' optimization in Brute Force algorithm skips characters in the pattern that are different from the corresponding characters in the text, reducing the number of comparisons required.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Brute Force algorithm without optimization?
cards.back: The time complexity of the Brute Force algorithm without optimization is O(n*m), where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.
